ip_outcome_note: Confirmed via PokemonTCG.io API (swsh5-69). National Pokedex no. 104. Artist: You Iribi. Visual match unambiguous — skull-masked brown creature with bone. Pokemon Company internal IP, no external crossover risk.

trivia_bullets.text: Common from Sword & Shield Battle Styles (swsh5), released March 19, 2021. Battle Styles introduced Single Strike and Rapid Strike mechanics. Set print total: 163 main cards (183 with secret rares). Regulation mark E.
Basic Fighting-type, 70 HP, evolves to Marowak. Attacks: Beat (Fighting, 10) and Headbutt (Fighting + Colorless, 30). Grass weakness. Single Colorless retreat. Artist: You Iribi.

"It wears its mother's skull on its head, so no one knows what its bare face looks like. However, it's clear that it's always crying." — Cubone is the Lonely Pokemon (National Pokedex no. 104). On full moon nights the skull vibrates and emits a mournful sound. Evolution into Marowak occurs when it comes to terms with grief; the skull then fuses to its head.
Lore paradox: Cubone eggs hatch with the skull already in place, creating an unresolved continuity loop despite Pokedex entries stating it wears its dead mother's skull.
sources: Bulbapedia: Cubone; Quora discussion, via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: Functions as an evolution setup piece for Marowak lines. Unremarkable competitively as a standalone attacker; its emotional lore resonance far outweighs its competitive utility.

related_cards.name: Marowak
related_cards.note: Direct evolution from Cubone; shares skull and bone iconography

related_cards.name: Marowak (Alolan)
related_cards.note: Regional alternate form; Fire/Ghost type; Alolan lore holds the bone is still its mother's

related_cards.name: Cubone
related_cards.note: Reprinted across multiple TCG generations; Battle Styles is the Sword & Shield era E-mark print
